reviews.review_description:
These programs have three major downsides from what I have experienced so far:

1) asset sizes/quality. Although the textures are low resolution, that is not my main concern here, as I understand tradeoffs are needed for it to run on Oculus Go. My issue is that the moose asset is way too small, it is distracting, and the cheetah asset appears to be kept at a distance specifically because it is mediocre across the board. A few others could be better but those two are the most annoying personally. 

2) walking area is very restricted on most programs, though the ocean and space ones it is a non-issue since visibility reduction and 3d movement make the ocean map feel larger, and the space map doesn't require movement. 

3) The program as a whole right now offers only a little more relaxation than a similar YouTube video at the moment.

On the plus side, I will say that all that said I do still use it frequently to calm down from a rough day. Individual experience will vary of course, but for those that can invest somewhat easily into VR the options do make some difference between this and the aforementioned YouTube. The space program is more of an ultralight universim. While overall relaxation is similar, controllability makes it enjoyable in a way beyond just YouTube. However, not 9.99 enjoyable.
Very relaxing. Highly recommended.

This was pretty much what I expected and is indeed a pleasant experience. My main issue was the limitation to the users area. There's clearly a LOD system in use  but surely as the user moves away you could de-spawn objects to replace them with 2d representations until out of sight, even in the Go.

This app is a bit crappy, by the time I had worked out the controls and what to do in it, then visit the other scenes hoping to be wowed I must have gone past 30 mins because I've just had my refund request rejected.  I really begrudge paying for this app as the free one is so much better and I will never click into this one again because its boring. Save your money on this one.

I am very happy with this new app on the Oculus Go, the environments are very absorbing, with plenty of wildlife. Unfortunately the tropical island is a bit of a let down, along with the space or universe theme, seems a bit pointless to me?
The savanagh wildlife is excellent, plenty of sound effects, the winter scenery gives a very good effect.
On the whole this is a good app, very enjoyable and one of my favourites!
Can I just say, the winter scene set to night and snowing, is fantastic, you really feel you are out there so much it almost chills your bones.
I love this app for relaxation. It had a temporary frustrating glitch with creator seeds that has since been fixed, and I am so glad because this is a great way to “get out” during quarantine.
I guess I’m not as easily pleased as others but the 90s computer game graphics seem pretty average

reviews.review_title: Great app but overheats too fast
reviews.review_description: I generally meditated for half an hour. It takes about eleven minutes to cause the go to give an overheating warning.

Update: I downloaded the app again onto my Oculus and tried the creator seed button - I was very happy that it worked!  I am able to manipulate the environment easily with my controller.  Thank you for fixing the function!  Overall, this is is great meditation app with excellent picture quality and 3d effects.  The environments are very immersive - I got startled once when a giraffe quietly walked up behind me.   I hope there are more worlds to explore in the future.  Thank you!
___________________________________________________________________________________________

Picture quality, graphics and sound quality are good.  However, the creator seed button does not seem to work on my Oculus Go even though I re-installed the app a couple of times.  It would be great if we could change the environment settings like adding rain/snow to some of the environments or having the option of changing day to night or night to day.  Also , it would be cool if the app adds a little optional guided meditation narratives into the mix.

This app is beautiful, I use it everyday to get my nature fix.I wish the developer extends the app by including more environments and increase the roaming area.
I am ready to pay twice for that

Nature Trek is an amazing experience that allows you to truly immerse yourself into various settings that you can control. I love everything about this experience along with the serene music. I could really spend much time with specifics but I will just state this; If you want peace and joy come visit these idyllic worlds.
I like it

This app is amazing! Relaxing and gorgeous landscapes. I love how the effects change depending on whether you pick day or night time. The creator seeds are a nice touch but the experience would be perfect even without them. 
I haven't tried all of them yet, but one of my favorite moments was traveling to the bottom of the ocean just to find a plane wreck that you could go inside, and sit in there and pretend that no one can find you 💜
Go to application for people who have never experienced VR.

I love the experiences Nature Trecks have to offer, but the enviorments, at least on the GO, are quite small. You can easily find walls and, at least on the GO, you can't really interact with the animals in any way. However, that being said, this is a very relaxing experince with a wide range of environments to experince. You can control the weather and time of day in most. They even have a space like enviornment with planets, black holes, sun's, and white dwarfs with gravity like effects. You can also fly in this enviorment wich is a lot of fun. This game never makes me dizzy or car-sick like most others and each enviornment gives my brain enough space to think and get creative. Almost like a meditation.
The music , locations are so relaxing. Very useful in these tough times where there’s no chance of going out

reviews.review_title: Amazing. I love this app. Soo relaxing..
reviews.review_description:
I really, really wanted a relaxation/chill/nature related app with environments I could just be inmersed in. 
I tried several relaxation apps, but mostly are very low resolution, or have flat 360 images, or bad inmersion. But Nature Treks VR was JUST what I wanted. As soon as I found it, I thought "Finally, this is it!". I've shown this app to several people, and everyone I've shown it too has been amazed by it.  Great, great app.

This app was very impressive👍. Love the realistic graphics & the thought of getting close to the deer was so realistic.  Love the nature graphics, music is soothing, this whole app has everything you need to “get away from it all”.  One of my very favorites.  Keep up the impressive work 🤗

I previously owned this on the Oculus Rift and was very happy with the immersive free roaming expansive settings so much that when I recently purchased the Oculus GO immediately paid for the same app thinking it would be the same experience. How wrong I was; the most glaring difference is the vastly shrunk environments now feeling more like I'm in a goldfish bowl with invisible walls stopping you very quickly in any direction. Why is this?  I understand differences in processing power between a PC and the GO but shrinking the environments has totally ruined the experience. Also there is a fair few glitches with non responsive inputs here and there. I could go on but this app has left me sorely dissapointed and cannot heartlly recommend this unless you've never seen the same app running in the RIFT.
One of my favorite apps, it's very relaxing
